_note_workflow_mode: Workflow mode controls the team interaction pattern. peer: symmetric review (both members audit independently). leader: leader produces step-by-step analysis, verifier checks each step. asymmetric: leader analysis with blind verification (critical steps redacted for verifier).
_note_project_stage: Stage controls strictness of some contracts. In exploration, the Capsule gate supports a minimal mode (see capsule.exploration_minimal) and selected preflight gates are downgraded to warn-only with debt recorded under team/runs/<tag>/<tag>_exploration_debt.md. Development remains fail-fast.
_note_scaffold_variant: Project scaffold size policy: minimal is now the canonical default surface; full adds optional wrappers/prompts/KB/references/team scaffolds when you intentionally want the research-team host-local bundle.
_note_review_access_mode: full_access allows members to request file reads/command runs/network fetches via the leader proxy with auditable evidence logs; packet_only restricts members to the team packet only (offline/portable review).
_note_isolation_strategy: Isolation is best-effort and enforced via the access proxy + evidence gates. separate_worktrees prefers isolated git worktrees when available; sequential_with_acl runs sequentially and denies reading the other member outputs.
_note_profile: Set profile to "" to derive from mode; otherwise overrides mode-derived profile.
_note_features: Leave features empty to use mode defaults. Set any key here to override a mode default.
_optional_claim_dag_gates: Claim DAG / Evidence gates (default off): claim_graph_gate, evidence_manifest_gate, claim_trajectory_link_gate.

claim_graph._note: Claim DAG / Evidence base directory relative to the project root.

knowledge_layers._note_require_min: Minimum counts apply after removing '- none' unless allow_none is false.

references._note: References-gate external link allowlist: keep stable by default; extend per project if needed.
references._note2: Default allowed hosts include INSPIRE/arXiv/DOI/GitHub plus common software/docs (SciPy/NumPy/Julia/PyPI/Zenodo).

logic_isolation._note: Independent reproduction scripts may import only these local project roots (default: shared_utils/toolkit).

independent_reproduction._note: Names of the kernel-under-test modules/packages. A reproduction that imports/includes/`using`s one of these is a call-through of the tested kernel, not an independent path (verdict not_independent, fail-closed). Declared kernels override allowed_local_import_roots. Independent of declarations, the gate also fails when BOTH members' reproduction sources import/include the same project-local module.

convention_mapping._note: Set required=true when a run depends on cross-paper/module normalization mappings; convention_mapping_gate enforces evidence.json fields when triggered.

markdown_math_hygiene._note: Deterministic scan targets for Markdown math hygiene gate (paths/globs relative to project root).

markdown_math_portability._note: Warn (default) on renderer-fragile math patterns in Markdown (domain-neutral).
markdown_math_portability._note2: Table policy: avoid literal '|' inside $...$ in Markdown tables; use \\lvert/\\rvert or \\lVert/\\rVert. Macro policy: prefer avoiding \\slashed in Markdown; use \\not\\! fallback if needed.

markdown_link_hygiene._note: Deterministic scan targets for Markdown link hygiene gate (paths/globs relative to project root).

latex_macro_hygiene._note: Disallow project-specific LaTeX macros in Markdown (rendering-safety). Prefer explicit forms like \mathcal{R}.
latex_macro_hygiene._note_forbidden_macros: Macro names without leading backslash. Gate flags occurrences like \Rc and suggests expansions.
latex_macro_hygiene._note_expansions: Deterministic macro expansions used by fix_markdown_latex_macros.py.

capsule._note_nontrivial: Require at least N Tier-T2/T3 headline(s) in Capsule section E (nontrivial diagnostics/proxies; avoids purely trivial arithmetic cross-checks). Set 0 only if truly N/A for a milestone.

exploration_minimal._note: When project_stage=exploration: relax the Capsule gate to avoid blocking early research. This is still deterministic and requires a non-empty reproduction command and at least one declared output path.
exploration_minimal._note_require_outputs_exist: If true: listed outputs must exist on disk (encourages running the command once). If false: missing outputs become warnings (useful for purely conceptual runs).
exploration_minimal._note_relax_env_and_sources: If true: missing hash/Manifest/numpy constraints in section F become warnings (still recommended).

prework._note: Prework is enforced via knowledge_layers_gate and the minimum requirements above.

automation._note: Auto-fill and auto-run controls for the autopilot loop.

kickoff_prompt._note: Generate a user-reviewable PROJECT_START_PROMPT.md before auto-run; optionally require approval.
kickoff_prompt._note_require_approval: If false: generate the prompt but do not block execution (useful for batch runs).

sidecar_review._note: Optional third reviewer for numerics-only audit (non-blocking).
sidecar_review._note_timeout_secs: If >0: after convergence accounting is recorded, wait at most this long for sidecar; then terminate it (warn-only).

member_a._note: Member A assignment strategy. Default host_native means the current host agent should use its official subagent mechanism. Shell runner scripts fail closed for host_native; set codex/claude explicitly only when a CLI runner is requested.
member_a._note_runner_kind: subagent is an alias for host_native. Use codex/claude only when a CLI compatibility runner is explicitly desired.
member_a._note_reasoning_effort: auto maps exploration/literature projects to medium and development/publication or leader/asymmetric work to high.

member_b._note: Member B assignment strategy. Default host_native means the current host agent should use its official subagent mechanism. Shell runner scripts fail closed for host_native; set gemini/claude/codex explicitly only when the user requests a provider-specific CLI runner.
member_b._note_runner_kind: subagent is an alias for host_native. Use codex/gemini/claude only when a CLI compatibility runner is explicitly desired.
member_b._note_reasoning_effort: auto maps exploration/literature projects to medium and development/publication or leader/asymmetric work to high.
member_b._note_claude_system_prompt: Optional alternate system prompt file for Claude runner (relative to project root). If empty, Member B uses --member-b-system.

_note_sidecar_reviews: Optional: run multiple non-blocking sidecar reviews (a small reviewer swarm). When sidecar_reviews is non-empty, it is used instead of sidecar_review. Each entry uses the same schema as sidecar_review.
_note_sidecar_reviews2: Optional per-entry keys: kinds_exclude (list of milestone kinds to skip; defaults to ["theory"]).

draft_review._note: Controls for TeX-source-first draft review packets (used by run_draft_cycle.sh).
draft_review._note_convergence: If true: run_draft_cycle.sh enforces a mandatory 3-party convergence gate (A/B/Leader) and exits non-zero when any report is not ready or has correctness-blocking issues.
draft_review._note_leader_system_prompt: Default Team Leader Draft Audit system prompt (Member C). run_draft_cycle.sh uses this when --member-c-system is omitted.
draft_review._note_focus_sections: Selectors (heuristic; not exact title matching): methods/results/physics. Use ["auto"] to use the default trio.
draft_review._note_focus_envs: Env allowlist for key extraction. ["auto"] expands to: math (equation, equation*, align, align*, gather, gather*, multline, multline*), algorithm/code (algorithm, algorithm*, algorithm2e, algorithmic, lstlisting, minted), theorem/proof (theorem, lemma, proposition, corollary, definition, proof).

claim_gate_auto_enable._note: Auto-enable Claim DAG gates after first convergence once knowledge_graph has minimal content.

pointer_lint._note: Set strategy to "" to use mode default. Otherwise overrides mode.

scan_dependency._note: If true: when a scan is detected but no rules file is found, gate fails (equivalent to --require-rules).

branch_semantics._note: If false: multi-root branch contract issues become warnings (non-blocking) even when branch_semantics_gate is enabled.
